channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This slick new service puts ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Wikipedia on the map
item.description: I don’t know about you, but I tend to think about my favorite tech tools as being split into two separate saucepans: the “classic” apps we’ve known and relied on for ages and then the newer “AI” apps that have shown up over the past several months to serve some super-specific purpose.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: The end of an era? TSMC, Broadcom could tear apart Intel's legendary business after 57 years by separating its foundry and chip design
item.description: Broadcom and TSMC reportedly looking to chop Intel in half, with separate interests in its foundry and chip design.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Theft of Financial Information via Card Skimming Machines Investigated in Massachusetts and Kansas
item.description: Massachusetts and Kansas authorities investigate the theft of residents' financial information using card skimming machines, affecting local supermarket chains and EBT cardholders.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: The Handmaid's Tale season 6: everything we know so far about the hit Hulu show’s return
item.description: The Handmaid's Tale season 6 will be available to stream on Hulu in April 2025 – here's everything we know about the dystopian drama's return, while we wait.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Amazon concedes that Chime SDK makes far more sense than the Chime application itself
item.description: Amazon’s announcement on Wednesday that it is abandoning its Chime collaboration app, while stressing that it will double down on the far more successful Chime software development kit (SDK), was an example of Amazon being Amazon. It knows what it does well, and where to focus.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Arizona advances bill to keep AI from rejecting medical claims
item.description: Arizona state lawmakers advanced a bill Thursday that would ban the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to deny medical claims. The Arizona House of Representatives passed the legislation 58-0, with two lawmakers sitting out the vote, and it now heads to the state senate for consideration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How artificial intelligence is energizing information technology
item.description: "Data is getting even more gravity in this space because the more data you have, the more accuracy these parameters in get in your model," said Bill Vass. The post How artificial intelligence is energizing information technology first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Meze Audio's beautiful new wired headphones have a new kind of planar magnetic driver, hand-finished in Ukraine
item.description: These new premium headphones from Meze Audio go big on planar magnetic drivers, with a suitably big price.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: North Korean hackers are posing as software development recruiters to target freelancers
item.description: Freelance software developers are being hit with infostealing malware to fund the North Korean regime, experts warn.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft’s new AI hub highlights apps that support NPUs
item.description: Applications that support the specialized AI chips on Copilot+ PCs have been rare, but Microsoft is looking to change that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ex-OpenAI CTO Mira Murati launches AI startup, recruits top talent from rivals
item.description: Former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati has launched an AI startup, Thinking Machines Lab, escalating the fight for top talent and reshaping competition in the fast-evolving sector.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft’s first step to scalable quantum computing
item.description: There’s a race going on. Not the one to build artificial general intelligence, whatever that may be, but to deliver the first at-scale quantum computer that’s able to solve a class of massive problems that would take far too long using even the most powerful supercomputer.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Playing by the rules of AI
item.description: The Fast Company Impact Council is a private membership community of influential leaders, experts, executives, and entrepreneurs who share their insights with our audience. Members pay annual membership dues for access to peer learning and thought leadership opportunities, events and more.
item.dc:creator: Juan Orlandini, Fast Company

item.title: Apple unveils a souped-up and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone
item.description: Apple has released a sleeker and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone in an attempt to widen the audience for a bundle of artificial intelligence technology that the company has been hoping will revive demand for its most profitable product lineup.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Smart home device maker exposes 2.7 billion records in huge data breach
item.description: Major IoT data breach: Mars Hydro database with 2.7 billion records exposed. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the breach is a reminder of the risks that come with using IoT devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More used Seagate HDDs disguised as new: Fraudsters make it tough to spot the difference
item.description: The controversy surrounding used Seagate hard drives has significantly widened, with fraudsters now targeting the company’s IronWolf and IronWolf Pro NAS drives alongside the previously affected Exos server-grade HDDs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 3 reasons to consider a data security posture management platform
item.description: A week rarely goes by without a major data security breach. Recent news includes a breach impacting an energy company’s 8 million customers, another compromising the information on 450,000 current and former students, and one more exposing 240,000 credit union members.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KRON4
